mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production directly correlates with contemporaneous corporate revenue expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days without requiring any threshold activation.
failure_condition: Walk-forward 20-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases fail to outperform those following decreases with t-test p > 0.05 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8%, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending expectations and compresses earnings forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE > 8%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The joint level of a high unemployment rate and a low federal funds rate captures a policy-response gap where monetary accommodation buffers equity downside during labor market stress, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days; the effect is absent when either variable is neutral.
failure_condition: The combined signal of high UNRATE and low FEDFUNDS produces negative mean 60-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year window, or the interaction term in a bivariate regression is not positive and significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During zero-interest-rate regimes, month-over-month increases in the consumer price index signal inflation risk without offsetting monetary response capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, positive month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes are followed by positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales signals robust consumer demand that immediately lifts near-term earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month increases are not followed by positive mean 5-day S&P 500 returns with t-test p < 0.05 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP contraction signals an economy-wide demand shortfall that depresses corporate profitability expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines relative to prior quarter
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 contractions coincide with positive mean 60-day S&P 500 returns, or the hit rate of negative returns falls below 55% over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls indicates deteriorating labor income and consumption capacity, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month PAYEMS declines are followed by positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in industrial production and increases in the unemployment rate jointly confirm a contracting business cycle, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days; either signal in isolation is insufficient to generate the same magnitude.
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of INDPRO declines and UNRATE increases fails to produce negative mean 60-day returns, or the interaction term coefficient in a bivariate regression is positive over any 3-year window.
